title: LSTM & Transformer Forecasting
short_description: Advanced time series forecasting with attention-based LSTM and Transformer, embargoed CV, and calibrated probability outputs
long_description: Advanced time series forecasting with attention-based LSTM and Transformer, embargoed CV, and calibrated probability outputs
technical_details:
## Algorithm & Strategy Structure

Two architectures compete on a 5-day forward return prediction task:

**LSTM with Attention**:
- 2-layer bidirectional LSTM (hidden_dim units each direction)
- Self-attention layer over the LSTM output sequence
- Linear head predicting 5-day return as a probability (up/down/neutral)

**Transformer (Encoder-only)**:
- 4 multi-head attention layers (8 heads), d_model = hidden_dim
- Positional encoding via learned embeddings (not sinusoidal)
- CLS token aggregation for classification

## Features (50+)

Price-derived: returns at 1/5/10/21/63 days, EWMA vols, realized skewness, Parkinson range vol.
Volume: OBV, volume z-score, VWAP deviation.
Technical: RSI, MACD histogram, Bollinger %B, ADX, ATR.
Macro: VIX level and change, credit spread, yield curve slope.

## Embargoed Cross-Validation

Purged K-Fold CV (López de Prado, 2018): a gap of `lookback` days is removed between train and validation folds to prevent information leakage through overlapping return windows.

## Calibration

Raw softmax probabilities are recalibrated using isotonic regression on a held-out calibration set, ensuring that predicted P(up) = 0.7 corresponds to ~70% empirical up-move frequency.

## Metric Methodology

- **Log-loss**: Primary training objective; measures calibration quality.
- **Directional accuracy**: Fraction of predictions where forecast direction matches realized 5-day return sign.
- **Information coefficient (IC)**: Spearman correlation between predicted rank and realized return rank, measured monthly.
- **Sharpe**: Backtest Sharpe from a simple long-if-p(up)>0.6, short-if-p(down)>0.6 strategy.
